name: Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop
name_original: Monte El Viejo y mirador de Casa Pequeña — circular trailrunning por la ribera del río Carrión
description: Jog the Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop, a moderate 8.6-mile route combining historic towpaths with challenging forest climbs and
region_intro: Jogging around Campos offers diverse landscapes, from coastal stretches to rural countryside and extensive trail networks. The region features renowned beaches like Es Trenc and Ses Covetes, providing scenic routes along the shore. Inland, the area is characterized by rural splendor, salt flats, and trails leading to viewpoints such as the Monastery of Monti-Sion. These varied environments provide a range of options for runners seeking different terrains and experiences. Best jogging routes around Campos The most popular jogging route is Sotillo de los Canónigos and Isla Dos Aguas Parks Loop, a 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail that takes 22 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a pleasant run through urban parkland. Another top favourite among local runners is Canal de Castilla Trail, a moderate 3.7 miles (5.9 km) path. This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, providing a flat and scenic running experience. Local runners also love the Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through Monte El Viejo and alongside the Canal de Castilla, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Jogging around Campos is defined by coastal paths, rural countryside, and extensive trail networks, offering options for easy, moderate, and challenging running experiences. The network includes routes suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 9 reviews. More than 1400 runners have used komoot to explore Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Campos area?
A: The Campos area offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 120 different trails to explore. These routes cater to all levels, from easy jogs to more challenging runs.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Campos?
A: Yes, Campos has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119458296/sotillo-de-los-canonigos-and-isla-dos-aguas-parks-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo de los Canónigos and Isla Dos Aguas Parks Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.2-mile (3.6 km) trail that takes about 22 minutes to complete, offering a pleasant run through urban parkland.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Campos?
A: Running trails around Campos feature diverse terrain. You'll find flat, scenic paths along waterways like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119449008/canal-de-castilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla Trail&lt;/a&gt;, as well as routes with moderate elevation changes through areas like Monte El Viejo. The region offers a mix of urban parkland, riverside paths, and natural environments.

Q: Are there any scenic running routes in Campos that offer natural beauty or points of interest?
A: Absolutely! Many routes in Campos offer scenic views and points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119582577/monte-el-viejo-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides beautiful natural scenery. Additionally, routes along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; offer picturesque views of this historic waterway and its structures, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Campos?
A: Yes, Campos offers several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119582577/monte-el-viejo-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8.6-mile (13.8 km) trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120117453/laguna-de-la-nava-circular-trailrunning-through-the-viewpoints-of-the-special-protection-area-for-birds" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava circular trailrunning through the viewpoints of the Special Protection Area for Birds&lt;/a&gt;, offering a unique experience.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Campos?
A: The running routes in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Runners often praise the variety of landscapes, from urban parks to scenic canal paths, and the well-maintained trails that cater to different fitness levels. Over 1400 runners have used komoot to explore the area's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Campos for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners looking for longer distances, Campos has several options. Another significant route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119412318/acueducto-de-abanades-circular-trailrunning-desde-osorno-la-mayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de Abánades — circular trailrunning desde Osorno la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 11.6 km.

Q: What attractions can I see near the jogging routes in Campos?
A: Beyond the running trails, the Campos area is rich in attractions. You can explore historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota of Astudillo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical landmarks in Campos?
A: Yes, several running routes in Campos offer glimpses of historical landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119420445/castillo-de-la-mota-astudillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Mota Astudillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you near the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota of Astudillo&lt;/a&gt;. The routes along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; also feature historical structures and scenic sections.

A: While specific parking locations for every trail are not detailed, many routes in and around Campos, especially those near towns or popular attractions like the Canal de Castilla, generally have accessible parking options. Q: What is a popular moderate running route in Campos?

This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, providing a flat and scenic running experience that is highly regarded.
tips: Valiaso natural space in the Palencia region and closely linked to its history. With an extension that reaches 1,435 hectares, it constitutes the great reserve of Palencia's biodiversity, its authentic green lung.
Viewpoint located in the natural space of Monte el Viejo that offers panoramic views of the city and its surroundings.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop offers a varied experience. You'll find flat, unpaved but well-suited towpaths (known as "sirgas") along the Canal de Castilla. As you ascend Monte El Viejo, the terrain transitions to wider, comfortable tracks that become more rugged and stony, including notably steep slopes. Descents from Monte El Viejo can be made via roads like the "Carretera del Caracol".

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The loop offers sections for various fitness levels. The Canal de Castilla parts are generally easy and flat, ideal for beginners. However, the ascent of Monte El Viejo includes quite steep slopes and can be demanding, making the full loop a moderate challenge. Beginners might prefer to stick to the canal sections or be prepared for a strenuous climb on Monte El Viejo.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a blend of historical and natural sights. Along the Canal de Castilla, look out for picturesque locks, docks, bridges, and historic flour mills. On Monte El Viejo, you can enjoy panoramic views of Palencia, serene pine forests, and moorland. Don't miss the Water Tower in Monte El Viejo and the stunning View of Palencia from Monte el Viejo Lookout.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail, and is public transport available?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the trail is easily accessible from Palencia. Many users start near the Dársena del Canal (Canal's dock) which is a common access point for both the Canal de Castilla and routes leading to Monte El Viejo. Public transport options within Palencia would likely connect you to areas close to the trail's starting points.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, especially on exposed sections, while winter might bring colder weather, though the paths are generally accessible.

faqs.question: Is the Monte El Viejo and Canal de Castilla Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop is generally dog-friendly. Both the Canal de Castilla towpaths and the natural areas of Monte El Viejo are popular for outdoor activities with pets. Always ensure your dog is under control, especially in areas with wildlife or other users, and be mindful of local regulations regarding leashes.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for Monte El Viejo or the Canal de Castilla?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access Monte El Viejo or to jog along the Canal de Castilla towpaths. Both areas are open to the public for recreational use.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop incorporates sections of several other well-known paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur), the GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal Sur), and the Camino Natural Vía Verde del Tren Secundario de Castilla. Additionally, the route intersects with local trails such as Camino R1 - La rampa, MTB4, and MTB3.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to refuel near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the loop is close to Palencia, you'll find various amenities in the city, including cafes, restaurants, and shops, perfect for refueling before or after your jog. Along the Canal de Castilla, some historic buildings might house small establishments, but it's always wise to carry your own water and snacks.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, wear comfortable jogging shoes suitable for both flat paths and potentially rugged, stony sections. Layers are recommended as conditions can change, especially when ascending Monte El Viejo. Bring sufficient water, particularly on warmer days, and consider sun protection. A map or GPS device is also helpful for navigation.

faqs.question: What makes this loop unique compared to other trails in the Palencia area?
faqs.answer: This loop stands out for its unique blend of historical significance and diverse natural environments. It seamlessly connects the 18th-century engineering marvel of the Canal de Castilla with the natural beauty and panoramic views of Monte El Viejo, offering a dynamic jogging experience that transitions from flat waterside paths to elevated, forested, and moorland terrains.
